title: Documentation
text:
1 Chapters
BCFMS-project-management is a Shiny app for a project management system for the MS core facility.
The MS Core Facility provides services such as ​
If you have any further questions regarding this repository, please do not hesitate to contact us @ omicsdesk!

title: Documentation
text:
1.1 How to render
quarto render Documentation/
This booklet consolidates all operational and technical documentation for the sequencing Shiny app.
TODO
split the docs into (for now) three parts

github-related (move sections from deploy-operations)
installation-related (server,shiny, apache2, etc.)
set up inside server.
???? deploy steps

title: 2  Quick Start
text: 2.1 Quick Start

title: 2  Quick Start
text:
2.1.1 Contents

Local dev — local mode
Local dev — LDAP simulation
Local dev — unset
VM — LDAP mode (production)
VM — local mode (maintenance)
VM — unset (clear overrides)


2.1.1.1 Local dev — local mode
Sys.setenv(APP_ENV = "dev", AUTH_MODE = "local")
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")


2.1.1.2 Local dev — LDAP simulation
Sys.setenv(
  APP_ENV = "dev",
  AUTH_MODE = "ldap",
  DEV_REMOTE_USER = "your_username",
  LDAP_URI = "ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636",
  LDAP_BASE_DN = "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de"
)
shiny::runApp("sequencing-app")
To run this locally, replace “your_username” with actual user name.


2.1.1.3 Local dev — unset
Sys.unsetenv(c(
  "APP_ENV","AUTH_MODE","DEV_REMOTE_USER",
  "LDAP_BIND_DN","LDAP_BIND_PW",
  "LDAP_URI","LDAP_BASE_DN","LDAP_DEBUG"
))


2.1.1.4 VM — LDAP mode (production)
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


2.1.1.5 VM — local mode (maintenance)
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=local
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


2.1.1.6 VM — unset (clear overrides)
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
# remove AUTH_MODE/LDAP_* lines, save, then:
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text: 3.1 Install all dependencies

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.1.1 Install packages
This should be done at the beginning after the VM was set up by the IT.
mkdir -p /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh






TipThe scripts ends with



Manual steps still required:
- Install Shiny Server (.deb) if not present.
- Configure Apache vhost, LDAP, and systemd env vars.




3.1.2 Install Shiny Server
Download the latest Shiny Server .deb from Posit and install it:
# Example pattern (replace filename with the current version)
sudo apt install gdebi-core
wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-20.04/x86_64/shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo gdebi shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
Start and enable the service:
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server
sudo systemctl start shiny-server


3.1.3 Install Java + mail packages (required for email notifications)
The app sends emails via mailR, which requires Java / rJava. This is for email notifications, not for LDAP auth itself.
sudo apt-get install -y default-jdk
sudo R CMD javareconf
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages(c("rJava","mailR"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'


3.1.4 Install LDAP packages (Ubuntu + R)
sudo apt-get install -y ldap-utils libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev ca-certificates
These packages should already be installed via the installation script above, but just to make sure!

3.1.4.1 Configure OpenLDAP
The client trust in /etc/ldap/ldap.conf:
sudo tee /etc/ldap/ldap.conf >/dev/null <<'EOF'
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
EOF
Install the R LDAP package:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly=TRUE)) install.packages("remotes", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org"); remotes::install_github("liamgilbey/ldapr")'
Quick checks:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldap01.biochem.mpg.de:636 -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=yeroslaviz)" mail
sudo -u shiny Rscript --vanilla -e 'library(ldapr); cat("ldapr OK\n")'

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.2 Configure the Shiny app folder
sudo usermod -a -G shiny $USER
sudo mv /srv/shiny-server/ms-app /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup 2>/dev/null || true
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo chown -R $USER:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app

3.2.1 Clone repo and set app folder
sudo rsync -av /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management/ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.3 Configure Apache (test HTTPS redirect)
copy the apache2 config file
sudo cp /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management/scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf

3.3.1 Enable the site and restart Apache:
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ms-vm-shiny-443.conf # this creates symlink to sites-enabled
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2




We have to wait for the certificate to activate this.












Done so far. Below is all copied from BCFNGS











3.3.2 Initialize the SQLite DB
cd /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R


3.3.3 Deploy the app files:
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
./scripts/deploy.sh

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text: 3.4 Server Infrastructure

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.5 Modify Text (Announcements)
The post-login landing text is now admin-editable in the app UI (no code edit needed for content changes):

Log in as admin.
Open Administer Projects -> Manage Landing Text.
Select the panel:

Announcement
service_info (service/instructions panel)

Add/Edit/Delete/Reorder white text boxes.
Changes are published immediately and stored in SQLite.


3.5.1 Markdown-lite syntax
Use these formats in each box:

Bold: **important text**
Bullet list:

- first item
- second item

Numbered list:

1. first
2. second

Link: [@NGS](mailto:ngs@biochem.mpg.de)



3.5.2 Where it is stored

DB tables:

announcement_panels
announcement_items

App render + admin handlers:

/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/sequencing-app/app.R

Style classes (unchanged):

.announcement-panel
.service-panel
.info-box


If tables are missing, the app auto-creates them and seeds default content on startup.


3.5.3 SQL handling (moved)
All SQL database commands and explanations were moved to notes/sql-handling.qmd.
This includes:

DB rebuild procedure
Admin user promotion in LDAP mode
Budget holder updates (CSV vs DB)
SQLite inspection and maintenance commands



3.5.4 Install required system dependencies

sudo apt install -y libssl-dev libxml2-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev libudunits2-dev libgdal-dev



3.5.5 Changing hostname to fit the apache2 config file

# Change the hostname
sudo hostnamectl set-hostname ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de

# Verify the change
hostnamectl status

nslookup ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de
ping ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de
Server:     127.0.0.53
Address:    127.0.0.53#53

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de
Address: 10.33.0.60

PING ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de (10.33.0.60) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de (10.33.0.60): icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.079 ms
64 bytes from ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de (10.33.0.60): icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.074 ms

sudo systemctl restart apache2



3.5.6 Automatic start-up

sudo systemctl enable apache2
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
section: 3.6 Ubuntu Bare Server Setup HOWTO (Shiny + LDAP)
text:
3.6 Ubuntu Bare Server Setup HOWTO (Shiny + LDAP)

3.6.1 Restart communication (quick resume)
If I ever need to resume this work with a new AI tool or a new chat session:

Read Troubleshooting for common failures and fixes.
Read Deploy & Operations for the current deploy/validation steps.
Read Change Log for the latest configuration changes and decisions.



3.6.2 Base OS prep
sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade -y
Install common tools and libraries:
sudo apt install -y \
  git rsync curl ca-certificates \
  build-essential \
  libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev \
  sqlite3 libsqlite3-dev \
  ldap-utils \
  apache2 apache2-utils
If mail sending via mailR is needed, install a Java runtime:
sudo apt install -y default-jre
If LDAP-related R packages need OpenLDAP headers, install:
sudo apt install -y libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev


3.6.3 Install R
sudo apt install -y r-base
If you need a newer R version than the Ubuntu default, add the CRAN repo and install from there (optional).


3.6.4 Install Shiny Server
Download the latest Shiny Server .deb from Posit and install it:
# Example pattern (replace filename with the current version)
# wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-22.04/x86_64/shiny-server-<version>.deb
# sudo dpkg -i shiny-server-<version>.deb
# sudo apt -f install -y
Start and enable the service:
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server
sudo systemctl start shiny-server


3.6.5 Clone the repo
cd /home/<your-user>
git clone git@github.com:yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management.git


3.6.6 Configure the Shiny app folder
sudo usermod -a -G shiny $USER
sudo mv /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app.backup 2>/dev/null || true
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo chown -R $USER:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
Deploy the app files:
cd /home/<your-user>/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/deploy.sh


3.6.7 Install required R packages
From R (or R -q -e):
install.packages(c(
  "shiny",
  "shinyjs",
  "DBI",
  "RSQLite",
  "digest",
  "DT",
  "shinyWidgets",
  "mailR",
  "ldapr"
))
If any package fails, install missing system libraries and retry.


3.6.8 Configure Shiny Server environment variables
Recommended: systemd drop-in (production-safe).
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
Add:
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
# Optional debug (dev only):
# Environment=APP_ENV=dev
# Environment=LDAP_DEBUG=1
Then restart:
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Alternative: /home/shiny/.Renviron (simpler, less explicit).


3.6.9 Configure LDAP trust (OpenLDAP client)
Edit /etc/ldap/ldap.conf and ensure:
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
Test:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 \
  -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=USER)" mail


3.6.10 Configure Apache reverse proxy + LDAP auth
Copy the repo Apache config and enable required modules:
sudo cp /home/<your-user>/BCFNGS-project-management/mpiseq2/ngs-vm_01-main.conf /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/01-main.conf
sudo a2enmod proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel rewrite ssl headers ldap authnz_ldap
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


3.6.11 Validate the full flow
curl -Ik https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
# Expect 401

curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
# Expect 302 to /sequencing-app/?auth_user=USER

curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-testing-vm.biochem.mpg.de/sequencing-app/
# Expect 200

curl -I http://localhost:3838/sequencing-app/
# Expect 200


3.6.12 Routine updates
cd /home/<your-user>/BCFNGS-project-management
git pull origin main
./scripts/deploy.sh


3.6.13 Production hygiene

Use a service bind account if LDAP requires bind credentials.
Keep DB files out of git.
Keep Apache debug logs off unless diagnosing a problem.



3.6.14 References

notes/vm-troubleshooting-guide.md
Server_preparations.qmd

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text: 3.7 SSL Certificates

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.8 SSL Certificate (for HTTPS)
the new setting of the certificates. I have three files.
The private key I created when applying for the certificate. The other two were downloaded, when the emial came from Harica (the certificate provider).
the PEM is the pem file and the DER (issuer) is the chain file. Both of them are needed.
Setting for the readability of the files:
cd /etc/apache2/certificate/2026
-rw------- 1 yeroslaviz b_mcf 3272 Nov 10 15:58 private-key.pem # udo chmod 600 /path/to/private.key  # Readable only by root
-rw-r--r-- 1 yeroslaviz b_mcf 1545 Nov 14 12:12 NGS_2026_Issuer_ChainFile.cer # sudo chmod 644 /path/to/chain.crt  # Readable by all
-rw-r--r-- 1 yeroslaviz b_mcf 2796 Nov 14 12:34 NGS_2026_Cert.pem # sudo chmod 644 /path/to/certificate.crt # Readable by all
All the files are saved under /etc/apache2/certificate/2026.
Now I created soft links for each of these files.
sudo ln -s /etc/apache2/certificate/2026/private-key.pem /etc/ssl/private/2026_private-key.pem
sudo ln -s /etc/apache2/certificate/2026/NGS_2026_Cert.pem /etc/ssl/certs/2026_NGS_Cert.pem

title: 3  Setup & Infrastructure
text:
3.9 Firewall

3.9.1 Firewall
at the moment is not active, but maybe in the future.

sudo ufw allow 3838
sudo ufw allow 80
sudo ufw allow 443

title: 4  Git and Github
text:
4.1 Create a VM SSH key to connect to github
Instruction to set up the git and github repository connection.

title: 4  Git and Github
text:
mkdir -p ~/.ssh
chmod 700 ~/.ssh
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "<your-github-email>" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
chmod 644 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm.pub

title: 4  Git and Github
section: 4.2 Load key and pin it in SSH config
text:
4.2 Load key and pin it in SSH config
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm


Code
cat >> ~/.ssh/config <<'EOF'
Host github.com
  HostName github.com
  User git
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm
  IdentitiesOnly yes
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config

title: 4  Git and Github
text:
4.3 Add key in GitHub
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_bcfms_vm.pub
Copy the full output (public key) and paste it in GitHub. Do not paste the private key file.
Choose one:

Deploy key (read-only) on this repo: clone + pull only.

(We don’t use SSH key, since we don’t plan to push from the VM to github)
Where to paste:
Deploy key: GitHub repo BCFMS-project-management -> Settings -> Deploy keys -> Add deploy key.

4.3.1 Confirm GitHub access and pull latest code.
ssh -T git@github.com


4.3.2 Clone repository
cd /home/<user>/
git clone git@github.com:<user>/BCFMS-project-management.git
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
git pull --ff-only origin main

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text: 5.1 VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.1.1 Contents

Scope
Port strategy (recommended)
Prerequisites (both VMs)
GitHub connection on a fresh VM
bcf-vm: clean install
ngs-vm: parallel install next to perl system
Legacy data migration (perl-VM → shiny-VM)
Cutover and rollback
Post-install verification

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.2 Scope
This guide covers a full installation of the Shiny app on two Ubuntu 24 VMs:

bcf-vm: clean install, no HTTPS cert (self-signed OK for testing).
ngs-vm: already running the legacy Perl system; install the Shiny app in parallel, then redirect the public hostname when ready.

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.3 Port strategy (recommended)
Recommended for parallel operation on ngs-vm:

Shiny Server stays on :3838 (default).
Apache vhost for the new system listens on :8443 (HTTPS) or another high port (e.g. :1234 if you prefer).
Existing legacy system remains on :443.

Why 8443: it is standard for HTTPS on alternate ports and avoids conflicts.

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.4 Prerequisites (both VMs)
Run the requirement check script:
bash /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/check_requirements.sh
Install missing apt packages:
bash /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/check_requirements.sh --install
Install missing R packages (including rJava):
bash /Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/check_requirements.sh --install-r
The requirement check reports missing rJava/mailR and missing Java tools (java, javac) explicitly.
Manual steps still required after package install:

Install Shiny Server from the .deb package (see bcf-vm Step 2).
Install Java + R mail dependencies for email notifications (see bcf-vm Step 2b).
Install LDAP client/build dependencies + ldapr (see bcf-vm Step 2d).
Ensure the shiny user exists and app-folder permissions are correct (see bcf-vm Step 2c).
Configure Apache vhost, LDAP, and systemd environment (see bcf-vm Step 6, bcf-vm Step 7, and ngs-vm Step 2).

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
section: 5.5 GitHub connection on a fresh VM
text:
5.5 GitHub connection on a fresh VM
Before cloning on either VM, configure GitHub access.
Use these sections in Git & GitHub:

GitHub connection on VM (clone/pull/push)
Enable push from the VM
Repo setup on the VM

Minimum check before clone:
ssh -T git@github.com
If this fails, do not continue with clone/deploy until SSH access is fixed.

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.6 bcf-vm: clean install

5.6.1 1) Install base packages
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y git curl rsync apache2 apache2-utils openssl sqlite3 ldap-utils \
  build-essential libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev r-base


5.6.2 2) Install Shiny Server
Copy the .deb to the VM and install it:
sudo apt-get install -y gdebi-core
wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-20.04/x86_64/shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo gdebi shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb


5.6.3 2b) Configure Java + mail packages (required for email notifications)
The app sends emails via mailR, which requires Java / rJava. This is for email notifications, not for LDAP auth itself.
sudo apt-get install -y default-jdk
sudo R CMD javareconf
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages(c("rJava","mailR"), repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'
Apply the same on ngs-vm and bcf-vm.


5.6.4 2c) Create shiny user + app-folder permissions
Shiny Server runs the app as the shiny service user.
getent passwd shiny || sudo useradd --system --home /var/lib/shiny-server --shell /usr/sbin/nologin shiny
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo chmod 755 /srv/shiny-server /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo usermod -a -G shiny "$USER"
If you are configuring a different account, replace "$USER" with that username.


5.6.5 2d) LDAP packages (Ubuntu + R)
Install LDAP client tools and build dependencies:
sudo apt-get install -y ldap-utils libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev ca-certificates
Configure OpenLDAP client trust in /etc/ldap/ldap.conf:
sudo tee /etc/ldap/ldap.conf >/dev/null <<'EOF'
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
EOF
Install the R LDAP package:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages("ldapr", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")'
Fallback if CRAN install is unavailable on this VM:
sudo Rscript --vanilla -e 'if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly=TRUE)) install.packages("remotes", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org"); remotes::install_github("liamgilbey/ldapr")'
Quick checks:
ldapsearch -x -H ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636 -b "dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de" "(uid=<user>)" mail
sudo -u shiny Rscript --vanilla -e 'library(ldapr); cat("ldapr OK\n")'
If ldapsearch only works with LDAPTLS_REQCERT=never, your /etc/ldap/ldap.conf trust settings are incomplete or incorrect.


5.6.6 3) Create a self-signed certificate (test only)
sudo mkdir -p /etc/ssl/localcerts
sudo openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 \
  -newkey rsa:2048 \
  -keyout /etc/ssl/localcerts/bcf-vm.key \
  -out /etc/ssl/localcerts/bcf-vm.crt


5.6.7 4) Clone repo and set app folder
cd /home/<user>
git clone git@github.com:yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management.git
sudo rsync -av /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/sequencing-app/ /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app


5.6.8 5) Initialize the SQLite DB
cd /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R


5.6.9 6) Configure LDAP mode (systemd override)
sudo systemctl edit shiny-server
[Service]
Environment=AUTH_MODE=ldap
Environment=LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
Environment=LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
Environment=TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


5.6.10 6b) Ensure app runtime env is loaded (.Renviron)
On bcf-vm and ngs-vm, we observed that LDAP worked reliably only after setting app env in:

/srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron

Create/update this file:
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de:636,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de:636
LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
# LDAP_DEBUG=1   # only while debugging
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
This prevents the app from falling back to local-mode UI when systemd env is present but not visible inside the R worker.


5.6.11 7) Configure Apache (test HTTPS redirect)
Use the versioned template from the repo:

scripts/bcf.vm.conf

Copy it on VM:
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/bcf.vm.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/bcf-vm.conf
Template includes:

HTTP→HTTPS redirect (:80 → :443)
Scoped proxy to /sequencing-app/
LDAP <Location /sequencing-app/> auth block
Canonical auth_user rewrite and websocket rule

Enable the site and restart Apache:
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite bcf-vm.conf # this creates symlink to sites-enabled
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
section: 5.7 ngs-vm: parallel install next to perl system
text:
5.7 ngs-vm: parallel install next to perl system

5.7.1 1) Keep legacy system on :443
Leave the currently active Perl :443 site enabled (on our VM this is 02-testing.conf).


5.7.2 2) Add a parallel Shiny vhost on :8443 (or :1234)
Use the versioned template from the repo:

scripts/ngs-vm-shiny.conf

Copy it on VM:
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-shiny.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny.conf
Keep certificate paths unchanged in this file:

SSLCertificateFile /etc/ssl/certs/ngs-cert.pem
SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/ssl/private/2026_private-key.pem
SSLCertificateChainFile /etc/apache2/certificate/2026_Cert_binary.cer

Enable and reload:
grep -q '^Listen 8443$' /etc/apache2/ports.conf || echo 'Listen 8443' | sudo tee -a /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


5.7.3 3) Validate parallel access
curl -Ik https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:8443/
curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:8443/sequencing-app/
If hostname/port tests fail, validate locally against the vhost:
curl -kI -H 'Host: ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de' https://127.0.0.1:8443/
curl -kI -u USER -H 'Host: ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de' https://127.0.0.1:8443/sequencing-app/
If the app returns HTTP 500 with mailR/rJava errors on ngs-vm, apply this runtime fix:
sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk r-cran-rjava
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
sudo env JAVA_HOME="$JAVA_HOME" /usr/bin/R CMD javareconf
sudo env JAVA_HOME="$JAVA_HOME" /usr/bin/Rscript --vanilla -e 'install.packages("mailR", repos="https://cloud.r-project.org", INSTALL_opts="--no-test-load")'
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server


5.7.4 4) Switch between LDAP and local modes on VM
Primary reference: LDAP chapter - switch between LDAP and local modes
In production, switch modes by editing:

/srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron

Set LDAP mode:
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=ldap/' /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Set local mode:
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=local/' /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
Verify effective runtime mode from app logs:
LATEST=$(sudo ls -1t /var/log/shiny-server/sequencing-app-shiny-*.log | head -n1)
sudo tail -n 80 "$LATEST"

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.8 Legacy data migration (perl-VM → shiny-VM)
Use this workflow to export legacy projects from the perl system on ngs-vm and import into either bcf-vm or ngs-vm (Shiny DB).

5.8.1 Step 1) Export CSV on ngs-vm (perl PostgreSQL)
psql -U letunic -h 127.0.0.1 -d mpiseq2_db -c "\
\\copy (\
  SELECT \
    p.id AS legacy_id, \
    p.name AS project_name, \
    m.login AS login, \
    m.name AS user_full_name, \
    l.name AS group_name, \
    COALESCE(p.ref_genome_legacy, rg.genome) AS reference_genome, \
    COALESCE(p.sample_type_legacy, st.type) AS sample_type, \
    t.type AS type_label, \
    p.sequencing_length AS sequencing_length, \
    COALESCE(p.budget_group_legacy, bg.budget) AS budget_group, \
    p.sequencing_platform AS sequencing_platform, \
    COALESCE(p.sequencing_kit_legacy, p.sequencing_kit) AS sequencing_kit, \
    p.sequencing_index AS sequencing_index, \
    p.note AS note \
  FROM project.project p \
  LEFT JOIN people.member m ON p.user_id = m.id \
  LEFT JOIN people.lab l ON m.lab_id = l.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.ref_genome rg ON p.ref_genome_id = rg.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.sample_type st ON p.sample_type_id = st.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.type t ON p.type_id = t.id \
  LEFT JOIN project.budget_group bg ON p.budget_group_id = bg.id \
  ORDER BY p.id\
) TO '/www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.csv' CSV HEADER"


5.8.2 Step 2) Normalize UTF-8 (recommended)
sudo python3 - <<'PY'
src = "/www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.csv"
dst = "/www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.utf8.csv"
with open(src, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f, \
     open(dst, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
    for line in f:
        if "Ã" in line or "Â" in line:
            line = line.encode("latin1", errors="ignore").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
        out.write(line)
PY


5.8.3 Step 3) Move CSV to the target VM
If importing into bcf-vm:
# run on bcf-vm once
mkdir -p /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/exports/

# run on ngs-vm (source)
scp /www/mpiseq2/exports/legacy_projects.utf8.csv \
  yeroslaviz@bcf-vm:/home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/exports/
If importing into Shiny on ngs-vm (same host as perl system):
cp -R /www/mpiseq2/exports/ /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/


5.8.4 Step 4) Import into Shiny SQLite DB
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
cd /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
If /home/yeroslaviz is not traversable by shiny (permission denied), either:

quick fix:

sudo chmod o+x /home/yeroslaviz

safer fix (preferred): copy script + CSV to a service-owned path and run from there.

Then run import:
sudo -u shiny python3 /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/import_legacy_projects.py \
  --db /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
  --csv /home/yeroslaviz/BCFNGS-project-management/exports/legacy_projects.utf8.csv

sudo systemctl start shiny-server


5.8.5 Step 5) Verify import
sudo -u shiny sqlite3 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM projects WHERE status='Legacy project';"

sudo -u shiny sqlite3 /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db \
"SELECT project_id, project_name, responsible_user, status FROM projects WHERE status='Legacy project' ORDER BY project_id LIMIT 5;"
Notes:

Rebuild is not required for regular imports; new legacy_id rows are inserted, already imported IDs are skipped.
If you need a full refresh of legacy rows, rebuild or delete legacy rows first, then re-import.
Detailed SQL notes remain in Documentation/chapters/sql-handling.qmd.

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.9 Cutover and rollback

5.9.1 Cutover target
After cutover:

https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de serves Shiny on :443 (no port in URL).
Perl fallback stays available on https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:9443.



5.9.2 1) Pre-cutover safety backup
sudo mkdir -p /root/apache-backup-$(date +%F-%H%M)
sudo cp -a /etc/apache2/sites-available /root/apache-backup-$(date +%F-%H%M)/
sudo cp -a /etc/apache2/sites-enabled /root/apache-backup-$(date +%F-%H%M)/


5.9.3 2) Prepare vhost files
Use the versioned templates from the repo:

scripts/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf -> /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
scripts/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf -> /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf

sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management/scripts/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf


5.9.4 3) Enable the cutover routing
grep -q '^Listen 9443$' /etc/apache2/ports.conf || echo 'Listen 9443' | sudo tee -a /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel rewrite headers ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf
# Disable the old Perl :443 site (name can vary by VM):
sudo a2dissite 02-testing.conf || true
sudo a2dissite default-ssl.conf || true
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


5.9.5 4) Automated switch/rollback script (optional)
You can use:

scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh

Cutover:
cd /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh cutover
cutover auto-disables competing :443 sites (including default-ssl.conf when present).
Rollback:
cd /home/<user>/BCFNGS-project-management
./scripts/cutover_ngs_vm_ports.sh rollback
rollback re-enables the first available legacy :443 site in this order:

PERL_443_SITE (if provided)
02-testing.conf
default-ssl.conf



5.9.6 Rollback (manual)
sudo a2ensite 02-testing.conf
sudo a2dissite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2dissite ngs-vm-perl-9443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


5.9.7 DB lifecycle policy (important)

scripts/deploy.sh is code-only deployment and does not recreate sequencing_projects.db.
Restarting shiny-server does not delete DB content.
Rebuild is manual and only when intentionally running /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/setup_database.R.
Keep legacy CSV import as a separate operation, not part of deploy.



5.9.8 DB snapshot / rebuild / restore runbook
Snapshot:
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
Optional rebuild:
cd /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
Restore snapshot:
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db.bak.<timestamp> /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/sequencing_projects.db
sudo systemctl start shiny-server

title: 5  VM Installation Guide (ngs-vm & bcf-vm)
text:
5.10 Post-install verification
Run these checks after install:
curl -Ik https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de/sequencing-app/
curl -Ik https://ngs-vm.biochem.mpg.de:9443/
Confirm:

no-port hostname opens Shiny.
LDAP prompt appears and login succeeds.
Perl fallback is reachable on :9443.

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
text: 6.1 Deploy & Operations

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
section: 6.2 Take backups (app folder, SQLite DB, Apache config).
text:
6.2 Take backups (app folder, SQLite DB, Apache config).
sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db \
  /srv/shiny-server/ms_projects.db.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)
sudo cp /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf \
  /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)
The ms-vm-shiny-443.conf file is the latest config file, supporting both the LDAP system as well as the https certificate.

Deploy.

./scripts/deploy.sh

Restart services.

sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2

Smoke‑test login and app behavior.

curl -Ik https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/sequencing-app/

Roll back from backups if needed.

sudo rsync -av --delete /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMM/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2

6.2.1 VM rollout HOW‑TO (Git + deploy + safety)
This is the repeatable checklist I use when rolling out to a VM (first ngs-testing-vm, then ngs-vm).

6.2.1.1 Step 1: Make sure GitHub access works on the VM
Check which remote is set:
git -C /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management remote -v
If the remote is SSH (git@github.com:...), verify SSH access:
ssh -T git@github.com
If SSH works but git pull still says Permission denied (publickey), the VM key is not added to the repo. The fix is to add the VM public key as a Deploy Key:
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
Then add that key in GitHub: Repo → Settings → Deploy keys → Add deploy key (read‑only is enough for pull).


6.2.1.2 Step 2: Pull latest code
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
git pull origin main
If Git says divergent branches, I set merge as the default and pull again:
git config pull.rebase false
git pull origin main
If Git refuses because of the local SQLite DB file, I stop tracking the DB on the VM:
git rm --cached ms-app/ms_projects.db
git commit -m "Stop tracking local SQLite DB on VM"
git pull origin main
This does not delete the local DB file. It just removes it from Git so future pulls work.


6.2.1.3 Step 3: Production safety backups (fast rollback)
sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db \
  /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)
sudo cp /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf \
  /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)


6.2.1.4 Step 4: Deploy
./scripts/deploy.sh


6.2.1.5 Step 5: Restart and smoke‑test
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2
Then open the app URL and verify: - LDAP prompt appears - login works - app loads


6.2.1.6 Step 6: Rollback (if needed)
sudo rsync -av --delete /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMM/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
sudo systemctl restart apache2

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
text:
6.3 Deploy Scope and DB Behavior
scripts/deploy.sh is code deployment only:

syncs app files from repo -> /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
excludes ms_projects.db
restarts shiny-server
runs LDAP smoke tests (unless SKIP_SMOKE_TEST=1)

It does not:

rebuild the DB
import legacy CSV
run setup_database.R
overwrite landing text content (announcement_panels / announcement_items)


6.3.1 When to run deploy
Run deploy after changes to app code/config in:

/home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management/sequencing-app/

Do not use deploy for Apache-only changes; apply Apache config separately.


6.3.2 Restart vs rebuild

Restarting shiny-server does not remove DB content.
Rebuild is a manual, explicit action (setup_database.R) and should be done only when needed.
Landing text edited via Manage Landing Text is DB-backed and survives deploy/restart.



6.3.3 Safe DB snapshot around risky maintenance
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
Restore snapshot:
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db.bak.<timestamp> /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db
sudo systemctl start shiny-server

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
text:
6.4 LDAP Deploy Smoke Tests

6.4.1 Step 1: Deploy with built-in smoke tests
Run on VM:
cd ~/BCFMS-project-management
./scripts/deploy.sh
The script now:

Deploys app files to /srv/shiny-server/sequencing-app/
Restarts shiny-server
Runs LDAP smoke tests:

/sequencing-app/ returns 302 to canonical ?auth_user=<logged-user>
tampered ?auth_user=other is rewritten to ?auth_user=<logged-user>
final app load returns 200


It exits with non-zero status and prints remediation hints if any check fails.

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
text:
6.5 Backups (summary)
This project currently has three backup mechanisms:

Manual pre-deploy rollback backups (recommended before any deploy/maintenance): copy the app folder, the SQLite DB, and the Apache vhost config with a timestamp. See “Production safety backups (fast rollback)” above.
Manual in-app backup/restore (Admin UI): admins can download a .sqlite backup and restore it later (restore makes a pre-restore copy first).
Automatic bi-weekly backups: present in the app code but commented out / disabled by default.

Note:

scripts/deploy.sh syncs app code only. If you changed Apache config, apply/restart Apache separately, then run curl checks.


6.5.1 Step 2: Non-interactive usage (optional)
If you do not want password prompts:
LDAP_TEST_USER="yeroslaviz-test" LDAP_TEST_PASSWORD="YOUR_PASSWORD" ./scripts/deploy.sh
Skip smoke tests only when needed:
SKIP_SMOKE_TEST=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
Override target base URL:
PUBLIC_BASE_URL="https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de" ./scripts/deploy.sh

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
text:
6.6 Feature Branch Deploy (Test VM)
Use this when you want to validate a feature branch on bcf-vm or ngs-testing-vm before merging to main.

Switch to your feature branch on the VM and pull latest branch commits.

cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
git fetch origin
git checkout <feature-branch>
git pull --ff-only origin <feature-branch>

Deploy branch code to /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.

PUBLIC_BASE_URL="https://<vm-hostname>" ./scripts/deploy.sh

Validate app behavior on that VM (LDAP/local mode, forms, emails, admin actions).
After merge to main, switch VM back to main and deploy again:

cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only origin main
PUBLIC_BASE_URL="https://<vm-hostname>" ./scripts/deploy.sh
Notes:

scripts/deploy.sh does not rebuild or replace the SQLite DB.
DB-backed content (projects, landing text, additional costs) persists across deploy and restart.

title: 6  Deploy & Operations
text:
6.7 ngs-vm Cutover (443/9443)
Target state:

https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de -> Shiny on :443

This avoids TLS hostname mismatch (same hostname for both endpoints).

6.7.1 Step 1: Prepare vhost templates
Use these repo templates:

scripts/mscf-vm-shiny-443.conf

Copy to Apache:
sudo cp /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management/scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf


6.7.2 Step 2: Enable routing and switch traffic
grep -q '^Listen 443$' /etc/apache2/ports.conf || echo 'Listen 443' | sudo tee -a /etc/apache2/ports.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel rewrite headers ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2dissite default-ssl.conf || true
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2


6.7.3 Step 3: Validate
curl -kI https://ms-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -kI -u USER https://ms-vm.biochem.mpg.de/sequencing-app/
curl -kI https://ms-vm.biochem.mpg.de:9443/


6.7.4 Step 4: Rollback
sudo a2ensite 02-testing.conf
sudo a2dissite ngs-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2

text: 7.1 Change Log

Changes on 03.03.2026
